entries.summary: Creates one land mass, sized and positioned by its attributes.
entries.details: The most common way to place non-player territory — islands, a center land, resource-only landmasses, etc. For player starting areas, use create_player_lands instead, which takes the same attributes but distributes evenly across all players.

entries.summary: Creates one land per player, using the same attributes as create_land.
entries.details: Use this for player starting territory; use create_land for everything else (islands, neutral land, resource zones).

entries.summary: Sets this land's size as a percentage of the total map area.
entries.details: Mutually exclusive with number_of_tiles — use whichever is more natural for the effect you want. Percent scales with map size; an absolute tile count doesn't.

entries.summary: Sets this land's size as an absolute tile count, instead of a percentage.
entries.details: Mutually exclusive with land_percent. Useful when you want a land's absolute size to stay the same regardless of overall map size.

entries.summary: Assigns this land to a numbered zone, used later to scope terrain/object placement.
entries.details: Zones are how you keep, say, an island's terrain generation from bleeding onto the mainland — later create_terrain/create_object blocks can target or avoid specific zone numbers.

entries.summary: Places objects — resources, units, or buildings — on the map.
entries.details: Works bare (create_object GOLD places gold with default rules) or with a block of attributes constraining count, placement terrain, distance from players, grouping, and more.

entries.summary: Restricts object placement to a land tagged with a matching land_id.
entries.details: Tag a land with land_id inside its create_land/create_player_lands block, then reference that same number here to guarantee objects land only on that specific piece of terrain.

entries.summary: Restricts object placement to a specific terrain type.
entries.details: Common for making sure, e.g., gold only spawns on GRASS and not in water or on cliffs.

entries.summary: Begins a block of percent_chance-weighted random alternatives.
entries.details: Each percent_chance line inside gives one alternative's odds — they should sum to 100 across the block. Closed by end_random.

entries.summary: Begins a runtime conditional, evaluated during map generation.
entries.details: Distinct from the #ifdef preprocessor directive — if/elseif/else/endif are evaluated while the map generates (e.g. checking map size constants like HUGE_MAP), not before parsing.

entries.summary: The default land terrain — plain grass.
A gold resource node, mined by villagers.
